canonical_filename.mechanism: Fixed filename SKILL.md required inside a named skill directory

custom_filename.mechanism: Not applicable — Cline uses fixed SKILL.md canonical filename only

description.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: description (required) — tells Cline when to use this skill, max 1024 characters

display_name.mechanism: yaml frontmatter key: name (required) — must exactly match the directory name

providerExtensions.name: Supporting Files in Skill Directory
providerExtensions.description: A skill directory may contain optional subdirectories alongside SKILL.md: docs/ for additional documentation (advanced config, troubleshooting guides, platform-specific instructions), templates/ for config files or code scaffolding, and scripts/ for deterministic operations like validation or data processing. Cline loads these files on demand via read_file or direct execution; only their output enters the context window, not the source code itself. This allows skills to reference large reference material without loading it at startup.

providerExtensions.name: Description-Based Auto-Invocation via use_skill Tool
providerExtensions.description: When the user sends a message, Cline sees all available skills with their name and description (~100 tokens per skill). If the request matches a skill's description, Cline activates it by calling the use_skill tool, which loads the full SKILL.md body. The description field is therefore a trigger specification: it should start with action verbs, include trigger phrases, and mention specific file types, tools, or domains. Cline's invocation decision is made by the model based solely on the description text — there is no explicit user @-mention syntax.

providerExtensions.name: Per-Skill Enable/Disable Toggle
providerExtensions.description: Each skill has a toggle to enable or disable it without deleting the skill directory. Skills are enabled by default when discovered. This allows users to deactivate skills (e.g., disable a CI/CD skill during local development) without losing the skill definition.

providerExtensions.name: Global Skill Takes Precedence Over Project Skill
providerExtensions.description: When a global skill and a project skill share the same name, the global skill takes precedence. This is the inverse of typical scope-override behavior (where project overrides global) and is notable for syllago import/export conflict resolution. Documented as intentional: global skills are general-purpose while project skills are team-specific.
